title: Mechanize two critical patterns instead of trusting prose
action: bee-compounding step 4: the first-choice promotion target for a lesson is an EXECUTABLE CHECK, not prose — prose taxes every session preamble and relies on being read. Two of the patterns just written into critical-patterns.md are mechanizable, and one of them already fooled the orchestrator during this very feature. Add both as assertions to tests/test_lib.mjs. (1) NO CONTROL BYTES IN VENDORED SOURCE: assert that every skills/bee-hive/templates/**/*.mjs file contains no C0 control character other than tab, newline, and carriage return. Today lib/feedback.mjs joins sort-key fields with a NUL separator, which makes grep and ripgrep treat the whole file as BINARY and print nothing at all — not even a zero count — so a source-level drift guard silently matches nothing and a reader concludes a symbol has vanished. The fix in feedback.mjs is to switch sortKey's separator from U+0000 to U+001F (unit separator, not a C0 control the check bans — pick any non-C0 sentinel that cannot appear in the data, and say why in a comment). Both the assertion and the separator change belong to this cell. (2) EVERY ALLOWLIST FIELD OWNS A VALIDATOR, generalized: the structural guard added by evolving-7 asserts this for ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC specifically. Keep it, and add a sibling assertion that ENTRY_FIELDS is derived (not a literal) by checking that the module source contains no `ENTRY_FIELDS = [` literal assignment — the inverse of the drift guard that was removed under decision b8fe5c81, and this time it pins the CORRECT shape rather than the defective one. Note the irony explicitly in a comment: the previous guard pinned syntax and pinned the wrong syntax; this one pins the absence of the defective syntax, which is a different and weaker claim, so it is paired with the behavioral structural guard rather than replacing it. Do NOT weaken any of the 108 existing assertions.

artifacts.substantive: control-byte sweep over vendored template sources; assertion that ENTRY_FIELDS is derived, paired with the existing behavioral structural guard

key_links: the control-byte assertion exists because a NUL separator made grep silently match nothing during this feature's own review
sortKey's separator changes in the same cell, since the new assertion would otherwise fail on the file that motivated it
prohibitions: Never weaken, skip, or delete any of the 108 existing assertions
Never use a C0 control character as a data separator in vendored source
Do not replace evolving-7's behavioral structural guard with a source-grep — pair them
